description: Clear TP dump description with LH via FRA
raw_text: Just booked this last week and it still works as of March 2026. If you want to avoid LH's massive YQ on JFK-BKK, route via FRA as a ticketing point. Book on LH metal: FORCE LH:JFK-FRA / FORCE LH:FRA-BKK. The TP dump via Frankfurt eliminates the $580 YQ completely. I paid base fare + taxes only. YQ showed as $0.00 in the breakdown. This is a textbook TP dump — the ticketing point in FRA breaks the surcharge chain.

description: Carrier switch dump using QR to avoid BA YQ
raw_text: Found a nice carrier switch to avoid BA's brutal YQ on JFK-LHR. Instead of flying BA metal all the way, book the outbound on QR via DOH: FORCE QR:JFK-DOH-LHR. QR doesn't charge YQ on this routing. Then fly back on BA: FORCE BA:LHR-JFK. The BA return segment charges YQ but the QR outbound is clean. Net savings about $450 vs. booking BA roundtrip. Confirmed working, just ticketed yesterday.

description: Fare basis dump on LH with specific booking code
raw_text: Interesting find: LH has a specific fare basis YLOWUS that structurally excludes YQ on JFK-FRA-SIN routing. Book as FORCE LH:JFK-FRA-SIN BC=YLOWUS. This fare basis dump saves around $520 in fuel surcharges. The YLOWUS fare class has been consistently YQ-free for the past few months. Note: only works in Y class, not in business.

The BA/AA interline agreement on LHR-SYD has a quirk where YQ is waived when ticketed as an interline itinerary. Book as FORCE BA/AA:JFK-LHR-SYD with both carriers on the routing. The alliance rule between these oneworld partners eliminates the surcharge on the LHR-SYD segment. Savings of about $380. Been working for at least 6 months now.

description: Discussion about YQ but no specific pattern
raw_text: Can someone explain what YQ is? I keep seeing people talk about fuel surcharges and fuel dumps but I don't understand how they work. Is it possible to avoid YQ on Lufthansa flights? Any tips would be appreciated.

description: Post reporting a pattern no longer works
raw_text: Update: the LX fuel dump via ZRH on LAX-BKK is no longer working as of this week. Swiss has changed their surcharge rules and the TP dump through Zurich now shows full YQ of $490. Tried it three times with different dates and it's dead. The routing was FORCE LX:LAX-ZRH / FORCE LX:ZRH-BKK. RIP to a great dump that saved us $490 per ticket. Airline fixed this fuel surcharge trick.

description: Post with two different dump patterns
raw_text: Two working fuel dumps I want to share: (1) LH TP dump on ORD-NRT via FRA. Book FORCE LH:ORD-FRA / FORCE LH:FRA-NRT. The Frankfurt ticketing point eliminates ~$560 YQ. (2) KE carrier switch on LAX-ICN. Instead of booking KE direct, route via NRT on NH outbound: FORCE NH:LAX-NRT / FORCE KE:NRT-ICN. Korean Air's YQ is avoided on the NH segment. Saves about $320. Both confirmed working this month.

description: Very old post with no recent confirmation
raw_text: The SN fuel dump from JFK to BRU via a TP in CDG still works. Book FORCE SN:JFK-CDG / FORCE SN:CDG-BRU. Brussels Airlines charges $280 in YQ normally but this routing trick brings it to zero. Standard ticketing point dump.
note: Old post — should get lower credibility score

description: Completely unrelated post about hotel deals
raw_text: Just wanted to share an amazing Marriott deal in Bangkok — 5000 points per night at the JW Marriott Sukhumvit. Great location near BTS. Book directly through Marriott Bonvoy for best rate guarantee. Points + cash option also available.

description: Vague mention of YQ avoidance without specifics
raw_text: I heard there are ways to avoid fuel surcharges on European carriers. Something about ticketing points? Does anyone know if the YQ dump tricks still work for LH or BA? I'd love to save on my next transatlantic trip.
